flashcards.back: A statistical model that assigns a probability to a sequence of words, quantifying how likely that sequence is in a given language.

flashcards.front: What is the Markov Assumption in the context of N-grams?
flashcards.back: The assumption that the probability of a word depends only on a fixed number of previous words (its Markov history), not the entire sequence.
The problem that most possible word sequences (N-grams) are never observed in the training data, leading to zero probability estimates under Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of Smoothing?
flashcards.back: To adjust probability estimates so that no N-gram has a probability of zero, redistributing probability mass from frequent to infrequent/unseen events.
Perplexity measures how well a probability model predicts a sample. A lower perplexity indicates a better language model.
